mechanism_narrative: Persistently near-zero federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS at 0.08%) keeps the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows compressed, supporting a sustained upward drift in equity prices over a quarterly horizon as investors reach for yield in risk assets.
regime_hint: zero lower bound / accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns during near-zero rate periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below the psychologically significant 7.0% threshold, it signals labor market healing that increases consensus expectations for continued Federal Reserve taper progress and economic normalization, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as risk premia compress.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 7.0% for the first time in the current easing cycle
regime_hint: labor market recovery phase of expansion
failure_condition: In the 12 months following any UNRATE cross below 7.0%, 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the mean triggered return is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects broad-based consumer and residential investment strength that jointly forecasts corporate revenue upside; neither series alone reliably predicts returns, but their simultaneous positive momentum drives S&P 500 gains over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS+HOUST momentum signal underperforms either single-series signal in out-of-sample Rank IC over any 2-year window, indicating the interaction adds no incremental predictive value, or combined Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects improving manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate earnings momentum, which translates into short-horizon positive equity drift as traders price in stronger near-term earnings reports over the following trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following positive month-over-month INDPRO prints show no significant difference from returns following negative prints (t-test p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) gain exceeding a strong-growth threshold signals robust labor demand that boosts aggregate income and consumption expectations, driving S&P 500 gains over the subsequent month as analysts revise earnings estimates upward.
failure_condition: In instances where PAYEMS gains exceed +200,000, 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 50% of cases, or the mean triggered return is statistically indistinguishable from unconditional mean returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a zero lower bound regime with FEDFUNDS near zero and CPIAUCSL indicating subdued inflation (below 2% annualized), the combination of low rates and low inflation removes near-term tightening risk, jointly sustaining a quarterly equity rally that would not occur if either condition were absent (e.g., low rates but high inflation).
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.25% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year change below 2.0%
regime_hint: zero lower bound with low-inflation regime
failure_condition: During periods satisfying both trigger conditions jointly, 60-day forward SPX returns underperform the unconditional historical average, or Rank IC of the joint signal is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained uptrend in housing starts (HOUST) during a post-recession housing recovery regime signals durable improvement in household wealth and construction-linked employment, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon; this relationship is expected to be materially weaker or absent outside a housing-recovery regime.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns conditional on rising HOUST trend show Rank IC below 0.03 during the identified housing-recovery regime, or the direction of the relationship reverses when compared against a pre-recession baseline sample.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in UNRATE combined with a PAYEMS acceleration above trend signals a labor market inflection point that triggers a short-term speculative rally in equities within the following trading week, driven by algorithmic and discretionary traders front-running expected hawkish-to-dovish Fed commentary shifts; neither indicator alone produces this short-horizon effect.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines month-over-month AND PAYEMS month-over-month change exceeds +150,000
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions are jointly satisfied, 5-day forward SPX returns are not significantly different from the unconditional 5-day return distribution (p > 0.10), or the joint signal's Rank IC is lower than either single-series signal's Rank IC.
